title: Fix IndexError crash clicking answer before question loads
description: QuestionScreen's answer buttons are created visible+enabled in compose() and #question-container has no default-hidden CSS. Under Textual 6.11 there is a paint window where the default 'Answer 1-4' buttons are clickable before on_mount/generation populates current_answers. Clicking then hits self.current_answers[index] with an empty list -> IndexError. The keyboard handler action_select_answer guards bounds; the mouse handler on_button_pressed does not. Fix: hide #question-container by default in CSS (display:none; _display_question sets it visible only after populating answers) + add a bounds guard to on_button_pressed. Add regression test.
close_reason: Fixed + restyled; verified with tests and rendered screenshots

title: Marked-wrong answer strikethrough reads as misplaced horizontal bar
description: When climbing back to a previously-incorrect question, the answer the learner already ruled out is shown with .marked-wrong (opacity 0.4 + text-style strike). After the minimal restyle the option text is so faint that the strikethrough line dominates and reads as a stray/misplaced horizontal bar among the centered options. Fix: drop the strikethrough; signal the ruled-out answer with a muted rose tint + mild dimming instead.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced strikethrough with dim rose tint; verified via screenshot

title: Calm/minimal TUI restyle: muted palette, flat elements
description: Restyle the TUI to be clean, minimal, restrained: muted low-saturation palette (soft slate accent, muted sage/rose for correct/incorrect), flat list-like answer buttons (no chunky 3-line boxes, no fills), quiet borders (round, surface-tinted) or none, no filled magenta header bar, and remove saturated inline Rich markup ([bold magenta]/[bold green]/[bold red]/[yellow]) in favor of CSS-driven muted colors. Touch theme.tcss + inline markup in home/journey/progress/question screens. Preserve #question-container display:none (recque-gsf).
close_reason: Fixed + restyled; verified with tests and rendered screenshots

title: TUI loses session status on pause/resume/complete (detached LearningSession)
description: QuestionScreen opens a fresh SessionService() per action and passes a LearningSession that was created/loaded by a DIFFERENT, already-closed service — so it is detached from any db. pause_session/resume_session/complete_session set session.status and commit a db that does not contain the instance, so the status change is never flushed. This is the same cross-session class of bug recque-gr1 fixed for recque_web by sharing one borrowed db session. Surfaced while adding Pilot tests for recque-9d1 (the aggregate refactor). Two adjacent pre-existing bugs were ALREADY fixed in that work: (1) self.call_from_thread -> self.app.call_from_thread (Screen has no call_from_thread in Textual 6.11, so question generation crashed in the worker), and (2) sessionmaker now expire_on_commit=False (create_session returned an expired+detached instance, so save_progress crashed with DetachedInstanceError). This bead tracks the REMAINING status-persistence bug, which expire_on_commit=False does NOT fix (the instance still is not tracked by the committing session).
design: Fix by having QuestionScreen borrow ONE db session for its lifetime (SessionService(db) borrow pattern) instead of opening a new service per action, OR have the lifecycle methods merge the passed session into self._db before mutating. Overlaps recque-nm1 (thin the screen via services) — coordinate. Add a Pilot/integration test asserting status transitions actually persist (active->paused->active->completed).
acceptance_criteria:
- pause/resume/complete persist status to the DB (verified by reloading the row)
- A test drives the screen and asserts the persisted status, not just in-memory state

title: Remove claude_cli backend from AIClient; default to API
description: The claude -p (claude_cli) backend added in recque-p8f is a non-starter. Remove it from AIClient and config, delete its test, update CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, and drop the web app's RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli default. AIClient keeps anthropic/openai/mock and defaults to the API (anthropic if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY else openai, else mock).
close_reason: Removed claude_cli backend from AIClient/config, deleted its test, updated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, dropped web app's claude_cli default. Defaults to API (anthropic/openai/mock). 104 tests pass, touched files lint-clean.

title: Fix recque_web: broken SessionManager import + default to claude_cli backend
description: recque_web/app.py imports recque_tui.domain.journey.SessionManager which was deleted (replaced by application.session_service.SessionService). The web app fails to import. Swap to SessionService (drop-in: signatures match) and default the web app to RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli so AI calls use the claude -p CLI under OAuth.
notes: Fixed: (1) SessionManager->SessionService import swap (drop-in, signatures match). (2) Defaulted web app to RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli via os.environ.setdefault at top of app.py (before recque_tui imports; get_config caches a singleton). (3) Found + fixed a cross-session SQLAlchemy bug: complete_session/resume_session loaded the session from a foreign factory() db, so the service committed its own empty session and the status change was lost. Now share one db session (SessionService(db) borrow pattern). Verified: claude_cli generates skillmap+questions end-to-end via HTTP; pop/save_progress HTTP 200; complete/resume now persist (active->completed->active). 113 tests pass, ruff clean. NOTE: web app depends on uncommitted question_engine.py (LearningContext), models.py (explanation field), mock_generator.py changes.
close_reason: Web app runs on claude_cli; import + cross-session persistence fixed; verified e2e, 113 tests pass

title: Add claude -p CLI backend to AIClient for dev-mode use of Max subscription
description:
Currently AIClient picks between 'anthropic' (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), 'openai' (needs OPENAI_API_KEY), and 'mock'. Both API paths cost per-token. Add a fourth backend, 'claude_cli', that shells out to the locally-installed 'claude -p' CLI — which uses the user's already-authenticated Claude Code session (drawing from the Max subscription, or post-2026-06-15 from the new Agent SDK credit pool). Lets the user run recque in dev without paying per-token.

Background — Anthropic docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview) state:
> Starting June 15, 2026, Agent SDK and 'claude -p' usage on subscription plans will draw from a new monthly Agent SDK credit, separate from your interactive usage limits.

So 'claude -p' is the right tool: single-shot, non-interactive, uses Claude Code auth. Until 2026-06-15 it draws from the interactive Max quota; after that, a separate credit pool.

Important caveat: structured output. The 'anthropic' SDK path uses the tool-use trick for guaranteed Pydantic-validated output. 'claude -p' has no equivalent — must prompt for raw JSON and validate post-hoc, with a one-retry fallback for malformed responses.
design:
## Design sketch

### Backend selection

Add a new env var RECQUE_BACKEND that explicitly overrides auto-detection. Order of resolution:

1. Explicit: RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli|anthropic|openai|mock
2. Auto-detect: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → anthropic, else OPENAI_API_KEY → openai, else mock
3. (claude_cli is never auto-selected — must be explicit, since 'claude' CLI being on PATH doesn't imply the user wants to use it)

### New backend method in recque_tui/core/ai_client.py

```python
import json, subprocess
from pydantic import ValidationError

CLAUDE_CLI_SYSTEM = '''Respond with a single JSON object that matches this schema. No prose, no markdown fences — JSON only.'''

def _generate_claude_cli(self, prompt: str, response_format: Type[T]) -> T:
    schema = response_format.model_json_schema()
    augmented = (
        f'{prompt}\n\n'
        f'{CLAUDE_CLI_SYSTEM}\n\n'
        f'Schema:\n{json.dumps(schema, indent=2)}'
    )

    for attempt in range(2):  # one retry on parse failure
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ['claude', '-p', augmented, '--output-format', 'json'],
                capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            raise RuntimeError('claude -p timed out after 60s')
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            raise RuntimeError(f'claude -p failed: {e.stderr}')

        # 'claude -p --output-format json' returns {'result': '...', ...}
        envelope = json.loads(result.stdout)
        payload_text = envelope.get('result', '').strip()

        # Strip any markdown fences the model might add despite instructions
        if payload_text.startswith('```'):
            payload_text = payload_text.split('\n', 1)[1].rsplit('```', 1)[0].strip()

        try:
            return response_format.model_validate_json(payload_text)
        except (ValidationError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
            if attempt == 0:
                logger.warning(f'claude -p returned invalid JSON, retrying: {e}')
                continue
            raise RuntimeError(f'claude -p returned non-conforming JSON after retry: {payload_text[:200]}')
```

### Wire into generate()

Add 'claude_cli' branch with the same fallback ladder as the others (try → log error → fall back to mock).

### Concurrency caveat

question_engine.py:prefetch_simpler_questions spawns N parallel calls. With claude_cli, that's N subprocess spawns at ~200-400ms each, each loading the Node.js binary. Cap concurrency at 2 for the claude_cli backend (or accept the latency).

### Detection / health check

On __init__, when backend=claude_cli is requested, verify 'claude' is on PATH ('shutil.which') and fail-fast with a friendly message if not. Don't try to verify auth — let the first call fail naturally.

### Config

Add to recque_tui/config.py: a 'backend' field on Config; honor env var RECQUE_BACKEND. Document in README and CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- New backend 'claude_cli' selectable via RECQUE_BACKEND env var
- Shells out to 'claude -p ... --output-format json' and parses the JSON envelope's 'result' field
- Uses prompt-engineered JSON output (with schema in prompt) since no tool-use is available
- One retry on parse failure before bubbling up
- Fallback ladder: claude_cli → mock (NOT to anthropic/openai — point is to avoid API spend)
- Concurrency cap to prevent N parallel subprocess spawns from question_engine.prefetch
- Friendly error when 'claude' is not on PATH
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md updated with how to use the dev-mode backend
- Tests: subprocess.run is mocked; verify schema is passed in prompt, JSON parsing handles both bare JSON and ```json fenced output, retry behavior
notes: User vision: 'while recque is running in dev, route question-generation through the Claude I am already paying for.' This is a personal-use case, single-user TUI, low volume — fits the allowed Agent SDK / claude -p usage pattern (it is the user's own auth, not a third-party login flow exposed to others).
close_reason: Implemented claude_cli backend with subprocess shim, schema-in-prompt JSON parsing, one-retry on malformed output, mock-only fallback, and 9 unit tests. Verified end-to-end against Max subscription: real Sonnet-generated Question returned and Pydantic-validated. Docs updated in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.

title: Expand application service layer beyond SessionService
description: SessionService landed (recque_tui/application/session_service.py). Extend the application/ layer so question_screen.py becomes mostly presentation. Likely services: AnswerQuestionService (handle correct/incorrect, push/pop, prefetch), StartSessionService (skillmap + create_session), MasteryUpdateService. Screens call one service method per user action.
acceptance_criteria:
- New services live in recque_tui/application/
- question_screen.py no longer instantiates QuestionEngine directly for orchestration
- recque_web/app.py routes the question loop through the SAME services (no second copy of push/pop/prefetch/persist orchestration; no direct ORM-schema imports inside request handlers)
- The answer-a-question loop exists in exactly one place, called by both interfaces
- Each service has unit tests
- Mock generator path still works end-to-end
notes: Stronger justification than just thinning question_screen.py: the answer-a-question loop (QuestionEngine + LearningStack push/pop/prefetch + persistence) is currently DUPLICATED across both interfaces — recque_tui/ui/screens/question_screen.py (433 lines) AND recque_web/app.py each independently instantiate QuestionEngine, construct LearningStack, call push/pop, and reach directly into database.repositories + ORM schema (app.py even does inline CachedQuestion/SessionProgress/Skill imports inside request handlers). The web app is already a partial, slightly-divergent reimplementation of the TUI flow. An AnswerQuestionService/StartSessionService that BOTH interfaces call would collapse the two orchestration copies into one and make recque_web a thin adapter rather than a parallel implementation. Without this, a change to the questioning loop must be made in two files that can silently drift. Architectural audit 2026-05-28: core TYPES are isolated (core/ has no runtime dep on outer layers; the database import in question_engine.py is TYPE_CHECKING-only) but the core WORKFLOW leaked into both delivery layers.

title: Promote LearningStack into a Session aggregate
description: Promote core/learning_stack.LearningStack into a full Session aggregate that owns: skills list, current_skill_index, stack, and per-attempt counters (questions_answered/correct). Expose answer(question, choice) -> AnswerOutcome and advance_skill() with invariants enforced inside the aggregate. UI screens should call aggregate methods rather than mutate fields directly.
design:
# Session aggregate sketch (recque-9d1)

## Shape: COMPOSE, do not replace
New file core/session.py. `Session` WRAPS a LearningStack (composition) and owns: topic, skills, current_skill_index, _stack (LearningStack), questions_answered, questions_correct. LearningStack stays the stack mechanic (CLAUDE.md calls it the cleanest domain object); Session delegates stack serialization to it so stack_state_json shape is UNCHANGED (no migration). Aggregate stays PURE: no QuestionEngine, no DB, no UI. judge is pure (question.correct_answer == selected) so the decision lives in the aggregate.

## answer() returns an outcome enum, caller does I/O
```python
class Outcome(Enum):
    CLIMB_BACK = auto()      # correct, popped, previous question now on top
    SKILL_COMPLETE = auto()  # correct, stack emptied
    DRILL_DOWN = auto()      # wrong, prefetched simpler pushed, ready to show
    NEEDS_SIMPLER = auto()   # wrong, no prefetch -> caller must generate, then push_simpler()

@dataclass
class AnswerResult:
    is_correct: bool
    outcome: Outcome
    next_question: Question | None      # set for CLIMB_BACK / DRILL_DOWN
    prior_question_text: str | None     # set for NEEDS_SIMPLER (gen context)
    selected_answer: str | None         # set for NEEDS_SIMPLER

def answer(self, selected: str) -> AnswerResult:
    q = self._stack.peek()
    self.questions_answered += 1
    if q.correct_answer == selected:
        self.questions_correct += 1
        self._stack.pop()
        if self._stack.is_empty:
            return AnswerResult(True, Outcome.SKILL_COMPLETE, None, None, None)
        return AnswerResult(True, Outcome.CLIMB_BACK, self._stack.peek(), None, None)
    self._stack.mark_incorrect(selected)
    simpler = self._stack.get_prefetched(selected)   # FIX: read entry prefetch, not stale self.prefetched
    if simpler:
        self._stack.push(simpler)
        return AnswerResult(False, Outcome.DRILL_DOWN, simpler, None, None)
    return AnswerResult(False, Outcome.NEEDS_SIMPLER, None, q.question_text, selected)
```

## Other methods
- push_question(q, prefetched)  # fresh top-level question after generation
- push_simpler(q)               # the NEEDS_SIMPLER follow-up after generation
- advance_skill() -> bool       # index += 1, clear stack; False when topic complete
- start_skill()                 # clears stack (bounds checked by caller/is_complete)
- properties: current_question, current_skill, is_complete, depth, accuracy, marked_incorrect
- to_dict/from_dict OR expose .stack for SessionService.save_progress (which already takes stack+index+skills separately) -> keep that call unchanged, pass session._stack

## Latent bug this fixes
question_screen uses self.prefetched.get(selected) for drill-down, but self.prefetched is never updated after pushing a simpler question -> reads the GRANDPARENT entry stale. Mostly masked (answer texts differ -> None -> regenerate) but fragile. Aggregate reads stack.get_prefetched on the current entry. Same latent bug exists in recque_web/app.py.

## Call-site shrink
question_screen._handle_answer/_show_feedback/_prepare_simpler/_next_skill collapse to: outcome = session.answer(selected); switch on outcome.outcome for rendering + (NEEDS_SIMPLER) trigger generation. self.skills/current_skill_index/counters/stack/prefetched fields removed, replaced by one self.session. Target <250 lines. recque_web/app.py uses the SAME Session API (feeds recque-nm1 dedup).

## Test plan
New tests/test_session.py: correct->CLIMB_BACK, correct-empties->SKILL_COMPLETE, wrong+prefetch->DRILL_DOWN, wrong-no-prefetch->NEEDS_SIMPLER, advance_skill bounds, accuracy counters, round-trip serialization equals old LearningStack shape.
acceptance_criteria:
- Session aggregate has typed methods answer() and advance_skill()
- Recursive push/pop logic moves from question_screen into the aggregate
- LearningStack.to_dict/from_dict shape preserved (no migration of session_progress.stack_state_json rows)
- ui/screens/question_screen.py shrinks materially (target: under ~250 lines)
- Existing tests still pass; new tests cover the aggregate's invariants
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (2026-05-29). New core/session.py Session aggregate (compose-over-LearningStack, pure, Outcome enum + AnswerResult, single push_question(q, prefetched=None)). tests/test_session.py (10 tests) covers all four outcomes, advance_skill bounds, counters, serialization round-trip = old LearningStack shape. question_screen.py rewired onto the aggregate (433->391 lines): removed self.skills/current_skill_index/stack/prefetched/counter fields -> one self.session; deleted _prepare_simpler_question (folded into _show_feedback outcome switch). tests/test_question_screen.py adds 2 Textual Pilot tests driving the real screen through DRILL_DOWN/CLIMB_BACK/SKILL_COMPLETE and the NEEDS_SIMPLER->regenerate-keeps-prefetch path. 116 tests pass, ruff clean.

LINE TARGET: AC says <250; landed at 391. The residual is genuine presentation + generation/persistence orchestration (compose(), render/stats, the @work generate_* workers, create_session/save_progress calls) whose extraction is explicitly recque-nm1 scope ("question_screen.py no longer instantiates QuestionEngine directly"). 433->391 is the honest floor for the aggregate-only step; the <250 target carries to nm1. DO NOT close 9d1 as fully meeting the line-count AC without that note / user sign-off.

PRE-EXISTING BUGS the Pilot tests surfaced & this work FIXED: (1) self.call_from_thread -> self.app.call_from_thread in both @work methods (Screen has no call_from_thread in Textual 6.11; generation crashed in-worker). (2) get_session_factory sessionmaker now expire_on_commit=False (create_session returned expired+detached LearningSession -> save_progress DetachedInstanceError). A THIRD, deeper status-persistence bug (pause/resume/complete on a detached session never flushes) is NOT fixed here — filed as a separate P2 bug bead.

title: Left-justify answer alternatives + separator between question and answers
description: Answer buttons rendered centered because Textual honours content-align (not text-align) for Button labels. Set content-align: left middle so options left-justify to a common margin. Also add a quiet separator (border-bottom on .question-text) dividing the question from the answer list.

title: Clear remaining ruff lint findings (W/F/I/N)
description: Resolve the remaining ruff findings beyond F401: trailing/blank-line whitespace (W291/W293), f-strings without placeholders (F541), unsorted imports (I001), 2 unused locals (F841: analytics.skill_ids dead code, test_repositories.repo leftover), and 3 N806 idiomatic 'Session = sessionmaker' renamed to session_factory. Goal: ruff check . clean.
close_reason: All ruff findings cleared on active code; legacy excluded per project policy

title: Remove _uncategorized placeholder logic from QuestionRepository.save
description: QuestionRepository.save invents a placeholder Topic named '_uncategorized' and a Skill under it when skill_id is not supplied. A repository making domain decisions. Move that responsibility to the caller (likely the new QuestionEngine/AnswerQuestionService path) and have the repository require a valid skill_id.
acceptance_criteria:
- QuestionRepository.save no longer creates Topic or Skill rows
- Callers pass a real skill_id (or service ensures one exists first)
- Mock generator and live engine both still cache questions correctly
- Existing test_repositories.py tests still pass (may need to update test setup to create a Skill first)

title: Map ORM rows to domain objects at the repository boundary
description: Today SessionRepository.get_paused/get_active return SQLAlchemy LearningSession instances that leak into UI code (e.g. ui/screens/question_screen.py:30). Map ORM rows to domain objects at the repository boundary so the UI never sees ORM types. Likely need: a domain Session dataclass (separate from the ORM LearningSession table).
acceptance_criteria:
- Repositories return domain objects, not ORM rows
- UI screens import from recque_tui.domain (or .application), not recque_tui.database.schema
- ORM types stay inside recque_tui/database/
